name: Python
scope: An INTERMEDIATE Python developer, with 2-5 years experience, has a solid grasp of core programming concepts like data types, control flow, functions (including lambdas), and is adept with data structures such as lists, dictionaries, and sets. They are comfortable with object-oriented programming, able to create classes, implement inheritance, and understand polymorphism. They can manage modules and packages, use Python's standard library effectively, and handle third-party packages with tools like pip and virtual environments. Proficient in file operations and data serialization (JSON, CSV), they can interact with databases using SQL and ORMs like SQLAlchemy. They develop simple web applications using frameworks like Flask, consume and create APIs, and understand basic networking and concurrency (threading, multiprocessing). They write unit tests using unittest or pytest, debug code efficiently, and adhere to code quality standards like PEP 8 and best practices in documentation. Familiar with version control systems like Git, they collaborate using platforms like GitHub, participate in code reviews, and communicate effectively within a team. They can automate tasks with scripts, optimize code for performance, contribute to system design discussions, and improve existing systems while maintaining security awareness. Continuously learning, they stay updated with Python developments, seek feedback, and are capable of independent problem-solving on moderate-complexity projects.
long_scope:
An individual at the INTERMEDIATE proficiency level in Python is expected to have moved beyond the basics and can apply Python skills to solve real-world problems. They have a solid understanding of core concepts and can contribute meaningfully to projects. Below is a comprehensive scope of what an intermediate Python developer should be able to accomplish:

⸻

Core Programming Skills
	•	Data Types and Structures
	•	Proficient use of fundamental data types: integers, floats, strings, booleans.
	•	Advanced manipulation of data structures: lists, tuples, dictionaries, sets.
	•	Understanding of immutable vs mutable types.
	•	Control Flow
	•	Mastery of loops (for, while) and control statements (if, elif, else).
	•	Utilization of list comprehensions and generator expressions for efficient code.
	•	Functions
	•	Defining and invoking functions with positional, keyword, and default arguments.
	•	Understanding variable scope and lifetime.
	•	Use of anonymous functions (lambda).
	•	Modules and Packages
	•	Importing and organizing code using modules and packages.
	•	Familiarity with Python’s standard library modules (e.g., os, sys, datetime).
	•	Error and Exception Handling
	•	Using try, except, finally, and else blocks to handle exceptions.
	•	Raising exceptions and creating custom exception classes.

⸻

Object-Oriented Programming (OOP)
	•	Classes and Objects
	•	Creating classes with attributes and methods.
	•	Instantiating objects and accessing class members.
	•	Inheritance and Polymorphism
	•	Implementing single and multiple inheritance.
	•	Overriding methods and using super() to extend functionality.
	•	Understanding polymorphism and its application in Python.
	•	Encapsulation
	•	Using access modifiers to control access to class members.
	•	Understanding of getters and setters.

⸻

File and Data Handling
	•	File Operations
	•	Reading from and writing to text and binary files.
	•	Using context managers (with statement) for file operations.
	•	Data Serialization
	•	Working with JSON, CSV, and XML data formats.
	•	Serializing and deserializing data using json and pickle modules.
	•	Data Manipulation
	•	Using libraries like pandas and NumPy for data analysis.
	•	Performing data cleaning and transformation tasks.

⸻

Third-Party Libraries and Tools
	•	Package Management
	•	Installing and managing packages using pip.
	•	Understanding virtual environments (venv, virtualenv, conda) to manage dependencies.
	•	Popular Libraries
	•	Utilizing requests for HTTP operations.
	•	Implementing regular expressions with the re module.
	•	Basic plotting with matplotlib.

⸻

Web Development Basics
	•	Frameworks
	•	Building simple web applications using Flask.
	•	Understanding the basics of routing, templating, and handling HTTP requests.
	•	APIs
	•	Consuming RESTful APIs.
	•	Creating simple APIs to expose data or services.

⸻

Database Interaction
	•	SQL and NoSQL Databases
	•	Connecting to databases like SQLite or PostgreSQL.
	•	Performing CRUD operations using SQL queries.
	•	ORMs
	•	Using Object-Relational Mapping tools like SQLAlchemy to interact with databases.

⸻

Testing and Debugging
	•	Unit Testing
	•	Writing tests using unittest or pytest frameworks.
	•	Understanding test cases, test suites, and test runners.
	•	Debugging Techniques
	•	Using debugging tools or IDE features to step through code.
	•	Interpreting stack traces and logging output.

⸻

Version Control and Collaboration
	•	Git Proficiency
	•	Cloning repositories, committing changes, pushing, and pulling updates.
	•	Managing branches and understanding merge conflicts.
	•	Collaboration Tools
	•	Participating in code reviews using platforms like GitHub or GitLab.
	•	Documenting code changes and writing clear commit messages.

⸻

Code Quality and Best Practices
	•	PEP 8 Compliance
	•	Writing code that adheres to Python’s style guidelines for readability.
	•	Documentation
	•	Writing meaningful docstrings and inline comments.
	•	Generating documentation using tools like Sphinx.
	•	Refactoring
	•	Improving code structure and efficiency without altering external behavior.

⸻

Problem-Solving Skills
	•	Algorithmic Thinking
	•	Applying common algorithms and data structures to solve problems.
	•	Optimizing code for performance and scalability.
	•	Project Contributions
	•	Breaking down tasks into manageable components.
	•	Estimating time and resources required for development tasks.

⸻

Networking and Concurrency
	•	Basic Networking
	•	Understanding sockets and basic network communication.
	•	Working with higher-level network protocols.
	•	Concurrency
	•	Implementing threading and multiprocessing for concurrent execution.
	•	Understanding the Global Interpreter Lock (GIL) and its implications.

⸻

Automation and Scripting
	•	Task Automation
	•	Writing scripts to automate routine tasks (file management, data parsing).
	•	System Interaction
	•	Using Python to interact with the operating system and execute shell commands.

⸻

Security Awareness
	•	Secure Coding Practices
	•	Understanding common security vulnerabilities (e.g., injection attacks).
	•	Handling sensitive data securely (e.g., using environment variables).

⸻

Soft Skills and Teamwork
	•	Communication
	•	Effectively communicating technical concepts to team members.
	•	Continuous Learning
	•	Keeping up-to-date with the latest Python developments and libraries.
	•	Seeking feedback and actively improving coding skills.

⸻

Expected Tasks and Outcomes
	•	Develop Moderate-Complexity Applications
	•	Building applications that integrate multiple components (e.g., data processing pipeline).
	•	Maintain and Improve Existing Systems
	•	Debugging and fixing issues in existing codebases.
	•	Enhancing features and optimizing performance.
	•	Contribute to Design Discussions
	•	Participating in planning and architectural meetings.
	•	Providing input on technology choices and implementation strategies.

⸻

Tools and Technologies
	•	IDEs and Editors
	•	Proficient with tools like PyCharm, Visual Studio Code, or Sublime Text.
	•	Version Control Systems
	•	Comfortable using Git and platforms like GitHub.
	•	Databases
	•	Experience with relational databases (SQLite, PostgreSQL).
	•	Testing Frameworks
	•	Familiarity with unittest and pytest.
	•	Containers and Virtualization
	•	Basic understanding of Docker for containerization.

⸻

Mastery Over Concepts
	•	Efficient Coding
	•	Writing optimized and efficient code using appropriate data structures and algorithms.
	•	Modular Design
	•	Organizing code into reusable modules and packages.
	•	Error Handling
	•	Anticipating potential errors and writing robust code to handle exceptions gracefully.

⸻

Technical Expertise
	•	Independent Problem Solving
	•	Tackling assignments without constant supervision.
	•	Debugging Proficiency
	•	Quickly identifying and resolving code issues.
	•	Best Practices Application
	•	Implementing industry-standard practices in coding and development workflows.

⸻

Designing Systems and Processes
	•	System Design
	•	Contributing to the design of applications and systems.
	•	Process Improvement
	•	Suggesting and implementing improvements to development processes and workflows.

⸻

An intermediate Python developer is a valuable team member capable of handling a variety of programming tasks. They are not only able to write functional code but also contribute to the overall development process through effective communication, adherence to best practices, and a commitment to continuous learning.

name: Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG)
scope: A person with INTERMEDIATE proficiency level in Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) as a competency should be able to understand the end-to-end retrieve→generate loop, explain embedding vs. token space, dense-vs-sparse-vs-hybrid retrieval, cosine/dot/Euclidean similarity, chunk window/stride/overlap and trade-off RAG against in-context learning or fine-tuning. They can build ingestion pipelines that extract, cleanse, de-duplicate, PII-scrub and metadata-tag HTML, PDF, DOCX, TXT, JSON or relational sources, then keep them fresh via scheduled or event-driven updates. They select embedding models such as OpenAI text-embedding-3, Cohere, Sentence-Transformers, MiniLM, handle batching, caching, OPQ/PCA dimensionality reduction, monitor drift and trigger re-embedding. They create FAISS, Milvus, Pinecone, Weaviate, Qdrant, Elastic k-NN or Azure Cognitive Search indexes, tune IVF/HNSW, shard/replicate, and fuse BM25 with dense vectors using Reciprocal Rank Fusion. They orchestrate retrieval with LangChain, LlamaIndex, Semantic-Kernel, employing self-query, multi-vector, ColBERT/BGE rerankers, query rewriting, multi-hop reasoning and token-budget-aware summarisation. They craft prompts with stuffing, refine, tree-of-thought or router patterns, incorporate metadata, chain-of-thought, function-calling, JSON mode, guardrails and citation style. They integrate GPT-4/3.5-turbo, Claude, Llama-2/3, Mistral, Gemma via SDKs, tune temperature/top-p/logit-bias, stream responses, emit canonical JSON/Markdown/HTML and highlight source spans. They evaluate with recall@k, precision@k, MRR, nDCG, FACTScore, ROUGE, BLEU, toxicity and coherence checks, run A/B and canary tests, and manage human-in-the-loop reviews. They profile p95 latency across retrieval-rerank-generate, apply token estimators, caching, GGUF/QLoRA quantisation, and balance cost per answer against vector storage. They deploy via Docker on Kubernetes/ECS/Cloud Run with CI/CD that covers embedding regeneration, index migration, prompt versioning and observe with OpenTelemetry, Prometheus and Grafana. They enforce cite-then-generate, similarity thresholds, OpenAI moderation, Perspective API, Tigris/Guardrails.ai/Rebuff policies, maintain GDPR/HIPAA/SOC2 lineage and PII redaction throughout. They document architectures, mentor juniors, collaborate with data/ML/product/security teams, and track emerging ideas like RAG-fusion, DSPy, IndexGPT, Ragas and HELM-RAG.
long_scope:
------------------------------------------------------------
COMPETENCY SCOPE  
Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) – INTERMEDIATE (≈3–5 yrs.)
------------------------------------------------------------

1. Conceptual Foundations  
   • Demonstrates firm understanding of the two-stage RAG loop (retrieve → generate) and the rationale for separating long-term knowledge storage from parametric knowledge in LLM weights.  
   • Can articulate token/embedding space vs. vector space; understands influence of embedding dimensionality, distance metrics (cosine, dot-product, Euclidean) on recall quality and latency.  
   • Grasps trade-offs between dense, sparse and hybrid retrieval and can explain how each impacts LLM grounding, hallucination rate and latency.  
   • Understands the difference between in-context learning, fine-tuning and RAG, and when to choose which.  
   • Familiar with chunking theory (window size, stride, overlap) and its impact on context injection cost, factuality and retrieval precision.

2. Data & Knowledge Source Preparation  
   • Designs an end-to-end ingestion pipeline covering extraction, cleansing, deduplication, language detection, PII removal and incremental updates.  
   • Normalises heterogeneous data formats (HTML, PDF, DOCX, TXT, JSON, relational records) into chunkable text while preserving layout-dependent semantics (tables, code blocks, footnotes, version history).  
   • Sets up scheduled or event-driven re-indexing to guarantee freshness (SLA-driven).  
   • Performs metadata enrichment (document type, author, timestamps, tags, task-specific labels) for filterable retrieval.

3. Embedding Generation & Management  
   • Chooses appropriate embedding model class (OpenAI text-embedding-3, Cohere multilingual, Sentence-Transformers, domain-specific MiniLM, etc.) based on domain, language coverage, latency, licensing and on-prem vs. SaaS constraints.  
   • Implements embedding caching, batching and rate-limit handling.  
   • Applies dimensionality reduction (PCA, FAISS OPQ) for storage/search efficiency while monitoring fidelity loss.  
   • Evaluates embedding drift across model upgrades and triggers re-embeddings accordingly.

4. Vector / Hybrid Indexing  
   • Creates indexes with FAISS, Milvus, Weaviate, Pinecone, Qdrant, ElasticSearch k-NN, or Azure Cognitive Search; can weigh pros/cons re: cost, scale, filtering, HNSW vs. IVF, write amplification.  
   • Configures hybrid retrieval (BM25 + dense) using query expansion or Reciprocal Rank Fusion; sets thresholds, weights and fallbacks.  
   • Knows sharding, replication, and tenancy options to maintain SLA for 10^5-10^7 chunks (multilingual).  
   • Monitors vector index quality (recall@k, latency p95) and performs periodic re-clustering/re-training.

5. Retrieval Orchestration  
   • Implements structured retrievers (metadata-filtered, time-aware, semantic-reranked) using LangChain, LlamaIndex, Semantic-Kernel or custom orchestration.  
   • Introduces self-query, multi-vector (page-level + section-level), parent-child or cross-encoder reranking (ColBERT, BGE-reranker) to boost precision.  
   • Applies query transformation: user-query rewriting, pseudo-relevance feedback, role-conditioning.  
   • Handles multi-hop / multi-stage retrieval for compositional queries.  
   • Observes prompt length, token budgeting, summarises retrieved spans when needed.

6. Prompt Engineering & Augmentation Strategies  
   • Embeds retrieved context using dynamic templates (stuffing, map-reduce, refine, tree-of-thought or router approach).  
   • Designs system/instruction prompts separating role, knowledge, task, citation style.  
   • Applies retrieval-conditioned generation (RCG) to enforce source-grounded answers with citations.  
   • Uses guardrails: max-token control, JSON mode, function calling, type-checking on outputs.  
   • Implements chain-of-thought or decomposition prompts when retrieval results are complex/unstructured.

7. Generation & Post-Processing  
   • Integrates with hosted or self-deployed LLMs (OpenAI GPT-4/3.5-turbo, Claude, Llama-2/3, Mistral, Gemma) through appropriate SDKs, ensuring secure key management, request batching and streaming.  
   • Controls temperature, top-p, logit bias, presence/penalty to balance factuality vs. creativity.  
   • Normalises output: canonical JSON/YAML, Markdown, HTML; uses regex or structured validation libraries.  
   • Adds answer justification and source highlighting (span mapping) for UX layers.

8. Evaluation & Metrics  
   • Designs evaluation harness (unit tests + offline corpora) with metrics:  
       – Retrieval: recall@k, precision@k, MRR, nDCG  
       – Generation: faithfulness (FACTScore, F1-Grounded), ROUGE/BLEU when reference available, toxicity, coherence, answer completeness.  
   • Sets up human-in-the-loop review loops and writes annotation guidelines.  
   • Automates regression testing on new data drops, index rebuilds or model version changes.  
   • Implements canary deployments and A/B tests; logs token usage, latencies, error codes.

9. Optimisation & Cost Management  
   • Profiles latency across retrieval, re-ranking and generation; establishes p95 budgets and probate when breached.  
   • Implements token-count estimators, chunk size experimentation, and caching (retrieval + generation).  
   • Calculates cost per thousand tokens, cost per answer, vector storage vs. compute trade-offs; applies usage caps or graceful degradation.  
   • Employs mixed precision or quantised models (GGUF, QLoRA) for on-prem GPU optimisation.

10. Deployment & Operations  
   • Containerises pipelines (Docker, OCI) and orchestrates via Kubernetes, ECS, or serverless frameworks (Fn, Cloud Run).  
   • Builds CI/CD workflows incorporating embedding regeneration, index migration, prompt versioning and smoke tests.  
   • Secures endpoints: OAuth2, mTLS, VNET/VPC isolation; complies with data-residency policies.  
   • Implements observability stack (OpenTelemetry, Prometheus, Grafana) capturing retrieval/generation spans and tracing.

11. Governance, Safety & Compliance  
   • Enforces grounding: cite-then-generate policies, refuses unrelated queries (vector-similarity threshold, fallback intents).  
   • Applies PII redaction both pre-indexing and post-generation; integrates policy engines (Tigris, Guardrails.ai, Rebuff).  
   • Understands licensing of embeddings, LLM models and vector DBs; tracks data lineage for audits (GDPR, HIPAA, SOC2).  
   • Implements content filters (OpenAI moderation, Perspective API, custom classifiers) for toxicity, bias, defamation.

12. Collaboration & Communication  
   • Can partner with data engineers for ingestion, ML engineers for model fine-tuning, product teams for UX requirements, and security for compliance.  
   • Documents architecture diagrams, prompt versions, dataset schemas, failure modes and rollback procedures.  
   • Mentors juniors on chunking, index design, and prompt safety basics; reviews pull requests for RAG pipeline changes.

13. Forward-Looking Awareness  
   • Stays current on new retrieval architectures (DSPy, IndexGPT, LlamaIndex composable graphs), model fine-tuning methods (RAG-fusion, Natural-Language-Reranker), and emerging benchmarks (Ragas, OpenAI Evals, HELM-RAG).  
   • Can propose incremental adoption (e.g., switch to re-ranker, migrate to hybrid index, integrate local LLM) based on business constraints and tech maturity.

------------------------------------------------------------
An individual operating comfortably across these thirteen dimensions demonstrates INTERMEDIATE proficiency in Retrieval-Augmented Generation. They possess solid command over RAG fundamentals, can ship stable, well-evaluated RAG services at moderate scale, and are capable of troubleshooting, optimising and iterating without constant senior oversight.
